content:
Week 4 Quiz (Due by the end of the week.)
After you complete the readings, click Next to continue to the Week 4 Lectures.

Week 4 | Compare and Contrast Essay

Compare and Contrast Essays
A compare-and-contrast essay is a type of academic writing. In this essay type, a writer selects two subjects (objects, people, or places) and examines their similarities and differences. These lectures will guide you through the components. They will structure the essentials for crafting a compare-and-contrast essay, whether you have been writing for a while or are just beginning to write.


Long Description


There are two main ways to organize a compare-and-contrast essay:

Subject-by-subject: In this organization, you first discuss all the similarities between the two subjects. Then, discuss all the differences between the two subjects.
Point-by-point: In this organization, you alternate between discussing the similarities and differences between the two subjects.
No matter which one of the two organizational patterns you choose, your essay should include an introduction, body paragraphs, and a conclusion.
